data.title: Michael Saylor Indicates Potential Increase in Bitcoin Holdings
data.description:
Crypto Rover posted on X that Michael Saylor, the prominent Bitcoin advocate and co-founder of MicroStrategy, has suggested the possibility of acquiring additional Bitcoin. This announcement comes amid ongoing discussions about the cryptocurrency's market dynamics and potential future growth.

Saylor, known for his bullish stance on Bitcoin, has consistently emphasized the digital asset's value as a long-term investment. His company, MicroStrategy, has been one of the largest corporate holders of Bitcoin, frequently making headlines with its substantial purchases.

The hint at further acquisitions aligns with Saylor's strategy of leveraging Bitcoin as a hedge against inflation and economic uncertainty. As the cryptocurrency market continues to evolve, Saylor's comments may influence investor sentiment and market trends.

While specific details regarding the timing or scale of potential purchases were not disclosed, Saylor's remarks underscore his ongoing commitment to Bitcoin. The cryptocurrency community is closely monitoring these developments, anticipating possible impacts on Bitcoin's price and market activity.

data.title: Michael Saylor has released another Bitcoin Tracker update; he may disclose his accumulating data next week.
data.description: Strategy founder Michael Saylor has once again posted information about Bitcoin Trackers on the X platform, and may disclose data on his increased holdings next week.

data.title: Russia's Dependence on Foreign Fighters Grows Amid Ukraine Conflict
data.description: Russia is increasingly turning to foreign fighters to bolster its military efforts in Ukraine, according to UK Defence Secretary John Healey. Bloomberg posted on X that this shift comes as Russian forces face mounting losses that exceed their ability to replenish troops. The reliance on external combatants highlights the challenges Russia faces in sustaining its military operations in the ongoing conflict.

data.title: Indian police arrest six suspects in a week-long crackdown
data.description:
The Indian police arrested six individuals due to fraudulent activities, including investment fraud, loan app fraud, and part-time jobs scams.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/indian-police-arrest-six-suspects/

data.title: He Yi commented on an interview with the former head of listings at Binance: Chase was not a listing manager and had no decision-making power.
data.description: Regarding the recent trending interview with "Chase, former head of listing at Binance," He Yi posted on the X platform, stating: "He wasn't a listing manager, he was a Business Development (BD) person. Binance's listing team, which handles project analysis and due diligence, doesn't communicate directly with project teams. Contract terms are handled by the BD team, but they have no decision-making power. Therefore, each BD person needs to establish connections with countless projects. Listing and BD are handled by two separate teams with different reporting lines. Thank you for your attention. It's good to have different perspectives to help people make judgments; there's no need to over-interpret yesterday's decisions for today's outcome."

data.title: Bitcoin consolidates; Fidelity's Timmer flags $60K support
data.description:
Fidelity's Jurrien Timmer outlines a $60K–$75K support zone, while data cites a potential $55K wick, framing whether the Bitcoin bear market bottom is in.
source: https://coincu.com/news/bitcoin-consolidates-fidelitys-timmer-flags-60k-support/

data.title: Binance Defends Its Role in October 10 Market Crash
data.description:
At the February 12, 2026 session of Consensus Hong Kong 2026, Binance Co-CEO Richard Teng directly addressed the October 10, 2025 “10/10” market collapse, framing the event as a macro-driven liquidation cascade rather than a platform-specific breakdown. The crash triggered approximately $19.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/binance-defends-its-role-in-october-10-market-crash/

data.title: Bitcoin Futures Show Institutional Bullishness Amid Market Dynamics
data.description: US institutional investors continue to exhibit a bullish stance on Bitcoin, as evidenced by a higher annualized basis on CME futures compared to offshore options markets, where interest appears to be waning. According to NS3.AI, the recent decline in Bitcoin prices to $60,000 lacks evidence of being linked to threats from quantum computing. Instead, stocks associated with quantum computing are moving in tandem with Bitcoin, rather than inversely. These market dynamics suggest a broader decline in risk appetite affecting growth assets, with fears surrounding quantum computing likely fueled by market hype rather than genuine fundamental threats.

data.title: XRP Surges Over 8% as Ripple CEO Joins CFTC Advisory Committee
data.description: XRP's price experienced a significant increase of over 8% after Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse was appointed to the Commodity Futures Trading Commission's (CFTC) Innovation Advisory Committee. According to NS3.AI, this development marks a closer alignment between Ripple and U.S. regulators, potentially indicating a shift in the regulatory landscape for cryptocurrencies. The appointment has sparked growing institutional interest, underscored by Goldman Sachs' substantial $152 million holdings in crypto ETFs, which further supports XRP's upward momentum.

data.title: U.S. Lawmakers Urge CFIUS to Investigate UAE Investment in WLFI
data.description: U.S. Senators Elizabeth Warren and Andy Kim have called on the Treasury Department to investigate a $50 million investment by the United Arab Emirates in WLFI. According to Bitalk News, the transaction has connections to the Trump family, raising concerns about national security and foreign influence. The lawmakers are urging the Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States (CFIUS) to scrutinize the deal to ensure it does not compromise U.S. interests. The involvement of foreign entities in American businesses, especially those linked to political figures, has prompted heightened scrutiny from both parties in Congress.

data.title: Analysis: US institutions remain bullish on BTC, while overseas investors are withdrawing.
data.description: According to analysis by Greg Cipolaro, Head of Research at NYDIG, there is a divergence in sentiment between US institutional investors and offshore traders in the Bitcoin market. Currently, the annualized basis for CME Bitcoin futures is higher than that of the offshore exchange Deribit, indicating that US hedge funds and other institutions still prefer to pay a premium to maintain long positions, while interest in leveraged long-term exposure in the offshore market has clearly declined. Regarding previous market rumors that the "quantum computing threat" caused Bitcoin to fall to $60,000, NYDIG believes the data does not support this logic. Recent Bitcoin price movements have shown a positive correlation with quantum computing-related stocks such as IONQ and D-Wave, rather than an inverse divergence. If quantum computing did pose a targeted threat, related stocks should rise when Bitcoin falls. The current synchronized decline reflects a decrease in the market's overall risk appetite for long-term growth assets. Furthermore, Google Trends data shows that increases in related searches typically rise rather than fall with the price of Bitcoin, suggesting that the topic is more driven by market enthusiasm than panic selling. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Fidelity Global Macro Head: BTC falls to the $60,000 support zone, possibly marking the low point of this cycle.
data.description: Jurrien Timmer, Global Head of Macro at Fidelity, wrote on the X platform that BTC fell to $60,000 last week, falling within the support range he predicted months ago, when he stated that another four-year bull market cycle might have ended. For a BTC winter, the drop to $60,000 is relatively shallow, but as the commodity currency matures, its volatility should decrease. It's currently impossible to determine whether $60,000 is the bottom, but Jurrien Timmer believes this price may be a low point, and after months of consolidation, the next cyclical bull market will begin. Based on the mathematical patterns of past cycles, future volatility may eventually push BTC to new highs.

data.title: Morgan Stanley Expands Crypto Strategy With Dedicated Blockchain Engineering Push
data.description:
Morgan Stanley is scaling its digital asset strategy beyond exploratory pilots by hiring a Software Engineer, Blockchain & Tokenization as part of a broader effort to position the bank as a centralized platform for digital wealth infrastructure.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/morgan-stanley-expands-crypto-strategy-with-dedicated-blockchain-engineering-push/

data.title: Lagarde Advocates Investment Incentives Over Taxes to Prevent Capital Outflows
data.description: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde emphasized the importance of creating incentives for investments in Europe as a strategy to prevent capital outflows to other regions. Bloomberg posted on X that Lagarde suggested this approach as more effective than imposing taxes. Her comments highlight the ongoing discussions about how to maintain economic stability and competitiveness in the European market.

data.title: Malaysians Demand Arrest of Anti-Graft Chief Amid Allegations
data.description: Hundreds of Malaysians dressed in black assembled in the capital on Sunday, demanding the arrest of anti-corruption chief Azam Baki. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting reports that allege Baki had improper shareholdings and that there was abuse of power within the commission he oversees. The protest reflects growing public dissatisfaction with the handling of corruption cases in the country. Demonstrators called for accountability and transparency in the investigation of these allegations, emphasizing the need for integrity in public office. The gathering underscores the ongoing concerns about corruption and governance in Malaysia.

data.title: Solana's Rise: From Developer to Crypto Billionaire
data.description: Toly, a former software developer with a decade of experience at a web company, has become a pivotal figure in the success of Solana. According to NS3.AI, his journey from a developer to a crypto billionaire exemplifies the substantial wealth creation opportunities within the blockchain industry. Toly's story highlights the transformative potential of the crypto sector, showcasing how individuals can achieve significant financial success through innovation and strategic involvement in blockchain technology.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Iran's Nuclear Negotiations Exclude Uranium Enrichment Halt
data.description: On February 15, local time, Iranian parliamentary spokesperson for the National Security and Foreign Policy Committee, Ebrahim Rezaei, stated that the ongoing negotiations do not include discussions on halting or abandoning Iran's uranium enrichment activities, nor on transporting Iran's nuclear materials out of the country. According to Jin10, these topics have been accepted by the United States. Rezaei emphasized that the Geneva talks are unrelated to missile or regional issues, asserting that Israel is the primary concern in the region. He suggested that Israel's threats should be excluded from the Geneva negotiations and addressed separately with regional countries to ensure development, peace, and mutual interests. Rezaei highlighted that the Iranian negotiation team is prepared with a negotiation plan, but expressed skepticism about the outcome due to the United States' past record.

data.title: Steak 'n Shake, the American fast-food chain, will be giving out Bitcoin bonuses of $0.21 per hour to its hourly employees.
data.description: Steak 'n Shake, the American fast-food chain, announced that it will partner with the Fold app to provide Bitcoin bonuses to all hourly employees at its company-owned stores. Starting March 1, 2026, eligible employees will receive $0.21 worth of Bitcoin for each hour worked, with a two-year vesting period. Based on a 40-hour work week, employees could accumulate approximately $436.80 worth of Bitcoin annually. This initiative is part of the brand's Bitcoin strategy. Steak 'n Shake began accepting Bitcoin payments last year and included its sales revenue in its Strategic Bitcoin Reserve (SBR). On January 16th of this year, the company further increased its Bitcoin position by $10 million. Steak 'n Shake stated that accepting Bitcoin has not only increased same-store sales by 10% but also saved 50% on transaction processing fees. (MLive)

data.title: Hapag-Lloyd AG in Advanced Acquisition Talks with Zim
data.description: German shipping company Hapag-Lloyd AG has announced that it is in advanced discussions to acquire Israeli competitor Zim. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential merger between the two shipping giants. This acquisition could significantly impact the global shipping industry, as both companies are major players in the sector. Hapag-Lloyd AG's move to acquire Zim aligns with its strategy to expand its market presence and enhance operational capabilities. The talks are ongoing, and further details are expected to be released as negotiations progress.

data.title: RippleX Expands Asset Locking on XRP Ledger with Token Escrow
data.description: RippleX has introduced Token Escrow on the XRP Ledger, broadening the scope of conditional asset locking to include stablecoins and tokenized instruments beyond XRP. According to NS3.AI, this new feature allows regulated institutions to conduct conditional, compliance-friendly settlements directly on-chain, facilitating processes such as delivery-versus-payment and structured payouts. The adoption of this feature depends on issuers enabling escrow flags and integrating with the ecosystem. Additionally, XRPL's reserve model may lead to increased demand for XRP, driven by the growth of ledger objects.
